structured_payload_candidate.title: Proven ON/OFF Definition
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: All equipment with DI status point
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The term 'proven' means that the equipment's DI status point matches the state set by the equipment's DO command point.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Alarm Exit Hysteresis
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Each alarm shall have an adjustable time-based hysteresis (default: 5 seconds) and an adjustable percent-of-limit-based hysteresis (default: 0%) to exit the alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Apply adjustable time-based and percent-of-limit-based hysteresis before returning to normal.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Each alarm shall have an adjustable time-based hysteresis (default: 5 seconds) to exit the alarm. Once set, the alarm does not return to normal until the alarm conditions have ceased for the duration of the hysteresis. Each analog alarm shall have an adjustable percent-of-limit-based hysteresis (default: 0% of the alarm threshold, i.e., no hysteresis; alarm exits at the same value as the alarm threshold) the alarmed variable required to exit the alarm.

anchor_repair_reason: evidence_quote replaced with verbatim section heading line
judge_reason: Evidence only shows section header; lacks details to support summary.

structured_payload_candidate.title: VFD Minimum Speed Monitoring and Correction
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For each piece of equipment, the minimum speed shall be stored in a single software point. The active minimum speed parameter shall be read every 60 minutes via the drive's network interface. When a mismatch is detected, the minimum speed stored in the software point shall be written to the VFD and a Level 4 alarm generated.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Every 60 minutes, or when mismatch detected
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Read active minimum speed every 60 minutes; if mismatch, write correct value and generate Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Mismatch between drive's active minimum speed and software point minimum speed
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: For each piece of equipment, the minimum speed shall be stored in a single software point; in the case of a hard-wired VFD interface, the minimum speed shall be the lowest speed command sent to the drive by the BAS. See Section 3.2.1.2 for minimum speed setpoints. The active minimum speed parameter shall be read every 60 minutes via the drive's network interface. When a mismatch between the drive's active minimum speed and the minimum speed stored in the software point is detected, the minimum speed stored in the software point shall be written to the VFD via the network interface to restore the active minimum speed parameter to its default value, and generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Trim & Respond Request Definition and Software Points
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A 'request' is a call to reset a static pressure or temperature setpoint generated by downstream zones or air-handling systems. For each downstream zone/system, provide software points: Importance-Multiplier (default=1), Request-Hours Accumulator, System Run-Hours Total, Cumulative%-Request-Hours. Reset accumulators automatically after 400 run-hours or manually. Generate Level 4 alarm if Cumulative% Request Hours exceeds 70% and run hours exceed 40.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Provide the specified software points; accumulate request-hours; reset accumulators as specified; generate alarm for rogue zones.
configurable_values: System Run-Hours Total reset threshold (default 400 hours)
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Cumulative% Request Hours exceeds 70% and run hours exceed 40
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: A 'request' is a call to reset a static pressure or temperature setpoint generated by downstream zones or air-handling systems. These requests are sent upstream to the plant or system that serves the zone or air handler that generated the request. a. For each downstream zone or system, and for each type of set-point reset request listed for the zone/system, provide the following software points: 1. Importance-Multiplier (default = 1) ... 2. Request-Hours Accumulator. Provided SystemOK (see Section 5.1.19) is TRUE for the zone/system, every x minutes (default 5 minutes), add x divided by 60 times the current number of requests to this request-hours accumulator point. 3. System Run-Hours Total. This is the number of hours the zone/system has been operating in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode. 4. Cumulative%-Request-Hours. This is the zone/system Request-Hours divided by the zone/system run-hours (the hours in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode) since the last reset, expressed as a percentage. 5. The Request-Hours Accumulator and System Run-Hours Total are reset to zero as follows: i. Reset automatically for an individual zone/system when the System Run-Hours Total exceeds 400 hours. ii. Reset manually by a global operator command. ... 6. A Level 4 alarm is generated if the zone Importance-Multiplier is greater than zero, the zone/system Cumulative% Request Hours exceeds 70%, and the total number of zone/system run hours exceeds 40.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Runtime Points Definition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Two runtime points shall be defined for each equipment: Lifetime Runtime (not readily resettable) and Staging Runtime (operator resettable).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Define Lifetime Runtime and Staging Runtime points as specified.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Two runtime points shall be defined for each equipment: a. Lifetime Runtime: The cumulative runtime of the equipment since equipment start-up. This point shall not be readily resettable by operators. b. Staging Runtime: An operator resettable runtime point that stores cumulative runtime since the last operator reset.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chiller Fault Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault conditions include: safety shutdown alarm, chiller manually shut off, chiller status remains off 5 minutes after command to start, CHW isolation valve not open 90 seconds after command, CW isolation valve not open 90 seconds after command, or for 10 minutes CHWRT at least 5°F above setpoint and delta-T less than 3°F.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 2 alarm and trigger response.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Safety shutdown alarm; chiller manually shut off; chiller status off 5 min after start; CHW isolation valve not open 90 sec after command; CW isolation valve not open 90 sec after command; CHWRT ≥5°F above setpoint and delta-T <3°F for 10 min.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: ii. Chillers (a) Safety shutdown alarm condition either through network or hardwired alarm contact (b) Chiller is manually shut off as indicated by the status of the Local/Auto switch from chiller gateway, or (c) Chiller status remains off 5 minutes after command to start ... (d) CHW isolation valve feedback indicates valve is not open 90 seconds after valve is commanded open, or ... (f) CW isolation valve feedback indicates valve is not open 90 seconds after valve is commanded open, or (g) For 10 minutes, chilled water return temperature has been at least 3°C (5°F) above the CHWST setpoint, and delta-T across the chiller ... has been less than 2°C (3°F).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Economizer Disable Based on High Limit
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Economizer shall be disabled whenever the outdoor air conditions exceed the economizer high-limit setpoint as specified by local code. Setpoints shall be automatically determined by the control sequences based on energy standard, climate zone, and economizer high-limit-control device type selected by the design engineer.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Outdoor air conditions exceed high-limit setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Economizer shall be disabled whenever the outdoor air conditions exceed the economizer high-limit setpoint as specified by local code. Setpoints shall be automatically determined by the control sequences (to ensure they are correct and meet code) based on energy standard, climate zone, and economizer high-limit-control device type selected by the design engineer in Section 3.1.4.3 or 3.1.6.2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: SystemOK FALSE Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.summary: SystemOK shall be FALSE while the system is starting up or when enough of the system's components are unavailable to disrupt the ability of the system to serve its load. By default, Level 1 through Level 3 component alarms shall inhibit SystemOK. Level 4 component alarms shall not affect SystemOK.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set SystemOK to FALSE during startup or when sufficient components are unavailable; Level 1-3 alarms inhibit SystemOK, Level 4 do not.
configurable_values: Threshold for component unavailability (defined by design engineer)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: SystemOK shall be FALSE while the system is starting up (i.e., before reaching setpoint) or when enough of the system's components are unavailable (in alarm, disabled, or turned OFF) to disrupt the ability of the system to serve its load. This threshold shall be defined by the design engineer for each system. a. By default, Level 1 through Level 3 component alarms (indicating equipment failure) shall inhibit SystemOK. Level 4 component alarms (maintenance and energy efficiency alarms) shall not affect SystemOK.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Time-Based Suppression Default Delay Periods
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Thermal zone temperature alarms and reset requests
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Calculate a time-delay period after any change in setpoint based on the difference between the controlled variable and the new setpoint. Default: for thermal zone temperature alarms: 18 minutes per °C (10 minutes per °F) of difference but no longer than 120 minutes. For cooling requests: 9 minutes per °C (5 minutes per °F) but no longer than 30 minutes. For heating requests: 9 minutes per °C (5 minutes per °F) but no longer than 30 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Apply time-delay period based on difference between controlled variable and new setpoint.
configurable_values: Time delay per degree (alarms: 18 min/°C, requests: 9 min/°C)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Calculate a time-delay period after any change in setpoint based on the difference between the controlled variable (e.g., zone temperature) at the time of the change and the new setpoint. The default time delay period shall be as follows: a. For thermal zone temperature alarms: 18 minutes per °C (10 minutes per °F) of difference but no longer than 120 minutes b. For thermal zone temperature cooling requests: 9 minutes per °C (5 minutes per °F) of difference but no longer than 30 minutes c. For thermal zone temperature heating requests: 9 minutes per °C (5 minutes per °F) of difference but no longer than 30 minutes
